description: Networking and inter-application communication in TouchDesigner including OSC server/client, WebSocket DAT, NDI video streaming, and TDAbleton sync for Ableton Live integration.

techniques.name: OSC Server and Client
techniques.description: Open Sound Control (OSC) communication in TouchDesigner for sending and receiving parameter data, triggers, and messages between applications, instruments, and machines.
techniques.notes: OSC In CHOP receives continuous float values as CHOP channels. OSC In DAT receives any OSC message as table rows. OSC Out sends data. Default ports: 7000 (receive), 7001 (send). Firewall must allow UDP on chosen ports.

code.snippet:
# OSC Setup and Handling in TouchDesigner

# --- OSC Receiver Setup ---
def setup_osc_receiver(oscInDat, port=7000, local_address='0.0.0.0'):
    """
    Configure OSC In DAT to receive on specified port.
    Messages arrive as table rows: [address, args...]
    """
    oscInDat.par.port = port
    oscInDat.par.localaddress = local_address
    oscInDat.par.active = True
    print(f'OSC receiving on port {port}')

# --- OSC Sender Setup ---
def setup_osc_sender(oscOutDat, host='127.0.0.1', port=7001):
    """
    Configure OSC Out DAT to send to specified host:port.
    """
    oscOutDat.par.networkaddress = host
    oscOutDat.par.port = port
    print(f'OSC sending to {host}:{port}')

# --- Sending OSC Messages ---
def send_osc(oscOutDat, address, *values):
    """
    Send an OSC message with address and values.
    Values can be float, int, or string.
    """
    oscOutDat.sendOSC(address, values)

# Example sends
# send_osc(op('osc_out1'), '/td/position', 0.5, 0.3, 1.0)
# send_osc(op('osc_out1'), '/td/trigger', 1)
# send_osc(op('osc_out1'), '/td/label', 'hello')

# --- Receiving OSC Messages (onReceiveOSC callback) ---
# Place in 'Callbacks DAT' of OSC In DAT
def onReceiveOSC(dat, address, args, peer):
    """
    Called for each incoming OSC message.
    address: string like '/td/control'
    args: list of values
    peer: (host, port) tuple
    """
    # Route messages to different handlers
    if address.startswith('/td/position'):
        if len(args) >= 3:
            op('pos_control').par.value0 = float(args[0])
            op('pos_control').par.value1 = float(args[1])
            op('pos_control').par.value2 = float(args[2])
    
    elif address.startswith('/td/color'):
        if len(args) >= 3:
            op('bg_color').par.colorr = float(args[0])
            op('bg_color').par.colorg = float(args[1])
            op('bg_color').par.colorb = float(args[2])
    
    elif address.startswith('/td/trigger'):
        op('trigger_node').par.value0.pulse()
    
    # Log all messages for debugging
    print(f'[OSC] {address}: {args} from {peer}')

# --- Bidirectional OSC with TouchOSC or similar ---
def setup_touchosc_bridge(oscInDat, oscOutDat, tablet_ip, receive_port=8000, send_port=9000):
    """
    Configure for TouchOSC app communication.
    """
    setup_osc_receiver(oscInDat, receive_port)
    setup_osc_sender(oscOutDat, tablet_ip, send_port)
    print(f'TouchOSC bridge: receive={receive_port}, send to {tablet_ip}:{send_port}')

techniques.name: WebSocket DAT for Browser Integration
techniques.description: Using WebSocket DAT to create a real-time bidirectional communication channel between TouchDesigner and web browsers, Node.js servers, or other WebSocket clients.
techniques.notes: WebSocket DAT can act as both server (for browsers to connect to) or client (connecting to an external server). TD also has a Web Server DAT for HTTP REST APIs.

code.snippet:
# WebSocket DAT — Server Mode
# TD acts as WebSocket server; browsers connect to ws://localhost:PORT

# In the WebSocket DAT parameters:
#   Mode: Server
#   Port: 9090
#   Active: On

# --- Callbacks DAT for WebSocket DAT ---
def onConnect(dat, rowIndex):
    """
    Called when a client connects.
    dat.row(rowIndex) contains client info (address, port, etc.)
    """
    client_info = dat.row(rowIndex)
    print(f'[WS] Client connected: {client_info}')
    
    # Send welcome message
    dat.sendText('{"type":"welcome","message":"Connected to TouchDesigner"}',
                 clients=[rowIndex])

def onDisconnect(dat, rowIndex):
    print(f'[WS] Client disconnected: row {rowIndex}')

def onReceiveText(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes):
    """
    Called when text message received from a client.
    """
    import json
    try:
        data = json.loads(message)
        msg_type = data.get('type', '')
        
        if msg_type == 'control':
            # Map incoming data to TD parameters
            if 'x' in data: op('control').par.value0 = float(data['x'])
            if 'y' in data: op('control').par.value1 = float(data['y'])
            if 'color' in data:
                rgb = data['color']
                op('bg_color').par.colorr = rgb[0] / 255.0
                op('bg_color').par.colorg = rgb[1] / 255.0
                op('bg_color').par.colorb = rgb[2] / 255.0
        
        elif msg_type == 'trigger':
            op('trigger_event').par.value0.pulse()
        
        # Echo back acknowledgment
        dat.sendText(json.dumps({'type': 'ack', 'received': msg_type}),
                     clients=[rowIndex])
    
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        print(f'[WS] Invalid JSON: {message}')

# --- Broadcast state to all connected clients ---
def broadcast_state(wsDat):
    """
    Send current TD state to all connected WebSocket clients.
    """
    import json
    state = {
        'type': 'state',
        'fps': project.cookRate,
        'frame': absTime.frame,
        'param_x': op('control').par.value0.eval(),
        'param_y': op('control').par.value1.eval()
    }
    wsDat.sendText(json.dumps(state))  # no clients arg = broadcast to all

# --- JavaScript client example (for browser) ---
# const ws = new WebSocket('ws://localhost:9090');
# ws.onopen = () => ws.send(JSON.stringify({type:'control', x:0.5, y:0.3}));
# ws.onmessage = (e) => console.log('TD:', JSON.parse(e.data));

techniques.name: NDI Video Streaming
techniques.description: Network Device Interface (NDI) for low-latency video over LAN. Send and receive full-quality video streams between TouchDesigner instances, video mixers, and other NDI-compatible software.
techniques.notes: NDI requires all devices on the same network segment (or NDI Bridge for cross-subnet). NDI DAT lists available sources. NDI Out TOP requires a valid license for some features. NDI is near-zero latency on GigE LAN.

code.snippet:
# NDI Streaming Setup and Management

def list_ndi_sources(ndiDat):
    """
    NDI DAT lists all available NDI sources on the network.
    Returns list of (name, IP) tuples.
    """
    sources = []
    for row in range(ndiDat.numRows):
        name = str(ndiDat[row, 0])  # source name
        ip   = str(ndiDat[row, 1])  # IP address
        sources.append((name, ip))
        print(f'[NDI] Found source: {name} at {ip}')
    return sources

def setup_ndi_receiver(ndiInTop, source_name):
    """
    Configure NDI In TOP to receive from a named source.
    source_name: as shown in NDI DAT, e.g. 'WORKSTATION (Program)'
    """
    ndiInTop.par.source = source_name
    ndiInTop.par.active = True
    print(f'[NDI] Receiving: {source_name}')

def setup_ndi_sender(ndiOutTop, stream_name='TouchDesigner Output', group='public'):
    """
    Configure NDI Out TOP to broadcast on the network.
    stream_name: visible name in NDI discovery
    """
    ndiOutTop.par.streamname = stream_name
    ndiOutTop.par.group = group
    ndiOutTop.par.active = True
    print(f'[NDI] Broadcasting as: {stream_name}')

def auto_connect_ndi(ndiDat, ndiInTop, preferred_source_keyword='VMIX'):
    """
    Auto-connect to first available NDI source matching keyword.
    """
    sources = list_ndi_sources(ndiDat)
    for name, ip in sources:
        if preferred_source_keyword.lower() in name.lower():
            setup_ndi_receiver(ndiInTop, name)
            return True
    if sources:
        setup_ndi_receiver(ndiInTop, sources[0][0])
        return True
    print('[NDI] No sources available')
    return False

# Multi-instance NDI routing (e.g., stage distribution)
def setup_stage_distribution(outputs):
    """
    Configure multiple NDI Out TOPs for stage distribution.
    outputs: list of (ndiOutTop, stream_name) tuples
    """
    for ndiOutTop, stream_name in outputs:
        setup_ndi_sender(ndiOutTop, stream_name)

techniques.name: TDAbleton Live Sync
techniques.description: Bidirectional communication between TouchDesigner and Ableton Live using the TDAbleton MIDI Remote Script. Syncs tempo, triggers clips, receives parameter changes, and streams audio analysis.
techniques.notes: TDAbleton uses a Max for Live MIDI Remote Script. Install in Ableton's MIDI Remote Scripts folder. Communication is over OSC (port 7000 TD->Ableton, 9000 Ableton->TD by default). Ableton Link CHOP provides Ableton Link protocol for tempo sync without TDAbleton.

techniques.name: TouchIn / TouchOut for TD-to-TD Networking
techniques.description: Using TouchIn/TouchOut CHOP and DAT operators for direct peer-to-peer communication between TouchDesigner instances over a LAN, without OSC overhead.
techniques.notes: Touch In/Out operates over TCP. One instance acts as server (Touch Out), others connect as clients (Touch In). Supports CHOP, DAT, and TOP data. Useful for multi-machine setups with a single master TD controller.

code.snippet:
# Touch In/Out Networking Setup

def setup_touch_out_server(touchOutChop, port=7500):
    """
    Configure Touch Out CHOP as server.
    Remote machines connect to this machine's IP on the given port.
    """
    touchOutChop.par.port = port
    touchOutChop.par.active = True
    print(f'Touch Out CHOP serving on port {port}')

def setup_touch_in_client(touchInChop, server_ip, port=7500):
    """
    Configure Touch In CHOP to receive from a server.
    server_ip: IP address of the master TouchDesigner machine
    """
    touchInChop.par.networkaddress = server_ip
    touchInChop.par.port = port
    touchInChop.par.active = True
    print(f'Touch In CHOP connecting to {server_ip}:{port}')

# Multi-machine show setup
def configure_show_network(machine_role, server_ip='192.168.1.100'):
    """
    Configure network based on machine role.
    'master': sends data, 'client': receives data
    """
    if machine_role == 'master':
        setup_touch_out_server(op('touch_out_chop'))
        setup_touch_out_server(op('touch_out_top'), port=7501)
        print('Master: broadcasting CHOP and TOP data')
    else:
        setup_touch_in_client(op('touch_in_chop'), server_ip)
        setup_touch_in_client(op('touch_in_top'), server_ip, port=7501)
        print(f'Client: receiving from master at {server_ip}')
